project: Auto Trading MVP - LiveTrader with SafetyGuard
description: PaperTrader 기반 자동 트레이딩 MVP 구현: SafetyGuard(킬스위치+일일한도) + LiveOrderManager(브로커 주문 래퍼) + LiveTrader(실제 주문 실행) + 스케줄러 통합. 대상: KIS 해외주식. v2: 10회 검증 결과 반영

userStories.title: SafetyGuard 모듈 — 킬스위치 및 일일 한도 관리
userStories.description: As a live trader, I need a safety guard that enforces daily loss limits, trade count limits, and a kill switch so that catastrophic losses are prevented automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Create trading_bot/safety_guard.py with SafetyGuard class

Constructor params: initial_capital (float, required), max_daily_loss_pct=0.05, max_daily_trades=50, max_position_count=10, max_capital_per_position_pct=0.15, slippage_tolerance_pct=0.02, db=None (Optional[TradingDatabase]), notifier=None (Optional[NotificationService])

pre_order_check(symbol, side, amount, price, positions, capital) -> Tuple[bool, str] — checks in order: (1) kill switch active, (2) daily loss limit (_daily_pnl/initial_capital <= -max_daily_loss_pct), (3) daily trade count >= max_daily_trades, (4) len(positions with qty>0) >= max_position_count (buy only, skip for sell), (5) amount*price/capital > max_capital_per_position_pct. Returns first failing check as (False, reason_str). All pass -> (True, 'ok')
post_fill_check(requested_price, fill_price) -> Tuple[bool, float] — slippage_pct = abs(fill_price - requested_price) / requested_price. Returns (slippage_pct <= slippage_tolerance_pct, slippage_pct)

record_trade(pnl: float) — increments _daily_trade_count, accumulates _daily_pnl += pnl. If _daily_pnl / self.initial_capital <= -max_daily_loss_pct, auto-calls activate_kill_switch(f'Daily loss {_daily_pnl:.2f} exceeded {max_daily_loss_pct*100}% of {initial_capital}'). Called by LiveTrader AFTER each SELL order fills with realized PnL

activate_kill_switch(reason: str) — sets _kill_switch_active=True. If db: try db.set_live_state('kill_switch_active', 'true') and db.set_live_state('kill_switch_reason', reason) except AttributeError pass. If notifier: notifier.notify_error(f'KILL SWITCH: {reason}', 'SafetyGuard'). logger.critical(reason)
deactivate_kill_switch() — sets _kill_switch_active=False, updates DB state if db provided
is_kill_switch_active() -> bool — returns _kill_switch_active

On __init__: if db provided, try loading kill switch state: _kill_switch_active = db.get_live_state('kill_switch_active') == 'true'. Wrapped in try/except (AttributeError, Exception) for graceful fallback when DB-001 not yet deployed
reset_daily_counters() — resets _daily_trade_count=0, _daily_pnl=0.0, _daily_reset_date=date.today()
_check_daily_reset(): called at start of pre_order_check(). If date.today() != _daily_reset_date, calls reset_daily_counters()
All public methods have type hints and docstrings

Create tests/test_safety_guard.py with these specific tests: test_kill_switch_manual_activation (activate -> is_active=True -> deactivate -> is_active=False), test_daily_loss_auto_kill (initial_capital=10000, max_daily_loss_pct=0.05, record_trade(-600) -> kill switch activated), test_trade_count_limit (max_daily_trades=3, 3 trades ok, 4th rejected), test_position_count_limit (max_position_count=2, 2 positions ok, 3rd buy rejected, sell allowed), test_capital_per_position (max_capital_per_position_pct=0.15, reject order exceeding 15%), test_slippage_check (tolerance=0.02, 1% ok, 3% not ok), test_daily_counter_reset (change date -> counters reset), test_kill_switch_db_persistence (mock db.set_live_state called), test_kill_switch_notifier (mock notifier.notify_error called)
pytest tests/test_safety_guard.py -v passes
userStories.notes: v2 changes: (1) initial_capital added as required constructor param (was missing), (2) notifier param added for kill switch alerts, (3) DB access wrapped in try/except AttributeError for graceful fallback when DB-001 not deployed, (4) pre_order_check no longer takes initial_capital arg (uses self.initial_capital), (5) test cases made specific with exact values. SafetyGuard has NO dependency on LiveTrader or LiveOrderManager.

userStories.title: Database 스키마 확장 — 라이브 트레이딩 테이블 3개 추가
userStories.description: As a system, I need database tables for live trading sessions, orders, and persistent state so that live trading data is tracked and the kill switch survives restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: In trading_bot/database.py _init_db(), add 3 new CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements after existing llm_decisions indexes (after line ~208)

Table 1: live_trading_sessions — session_id TEXT PK, strategy_name TEXT NOT NULL, display_name TEXT, mode TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'dry_run' (NOT 'live' — safe default), start_time TEXT NOT NULL, end_time TEXT, initial_capital REAL NOT NULL, final_capital REAL, total_return REAL, sharpe_ratio REAL, max_drawdown REAL, win_rate REAL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', kill_switch_reason TEXT, broker_name TEXT, market_type TEXT

Table 2: live_orders — internal_id TEXT PK, session_id TEXT NOT NULL, broker_order_id TEXT, symbol TEXT NOT NULL, side TEXT NOT NULL, order_type TEXT NOT NULL, requested_amount REAL NOT NULL, requested_price REAL, filled_amount REAL DEFAULT 0.0, filled_price REAL DEFAULT 0.0, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', reason TEXT, submitted_at TEXT, filled_at TEXT, commission REAL DEFAULT 0.0, slippage_pct REAL DEFAULT 0.0, error_message TEXT. NO FK constraint (session may not exist yet during edge cases)
Table 3: live_trading_state — key TEXT PK, value TEXT NOT NULL, updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
Add indexes: idx_live_orders_session ON live_orders(session_id), idx_live_orders_status ON live_orders(status), idx_live_sessions_status ON live_trading_sessions(status)

Add method create_live_session(session_id, strategy_name, display_name, mode, initial_capital, broker_name=None, market_type=None) — INSERT into live_trading_sessions with start_time=datetime.now().isoformat()
Add method update_live_session(session_id, updates: Dict) — SET each key=value in updates WHERE session_id=?
Add method get_live_session(session_id) -> Optional[Dict] — SELECT * WHERE session_id=?, return dict(row) or None
Add method log_live_order(order_dict: Dict) — INSERT into live_orders. order_dict keys match table columns
Add method update_live_order(internal_id, updates: Dict) — SET each key=value WHERE internal_id=?
Add method get_live_orders(session_id, status=None) -> List[Dict] — SELECT * WHERE session_id=? AND optionally status=?
Add method get_live_state(key: str) -> Optional[str] — SELECT value FROM live_trading_state WHERE key=? LIMIT 1. Returns value string or None
Add method set_live_state(key: str, value: str) — INSERT OR REPLACE INTO live_trading_state (key, value, updated_at) VALUES (?, ?, datetime.now().isoformat())
All new methods use self._get_connection() context manager pattern (same as existing create_session, log_trade)
Existing tables and methods completely unchanged — additive only, no modifications
pytest tests/test_paper_trader_db.py -v still passes (no regression)

Create tests/test_live_trading_db.py with: test_tables_created (verify 3 tables exist in schema), test_create_get_live_session (create, retrieve, verify fields), test_update_live_session (update final_capital+status, verify), test_log_get_live_orders (log order, retrieve by session_id, retrieve by status filter), test_update_live_order (update status from pending to filled), test_get_set_live_state (set key, get key, overwrite key), test_live_state_returns_none_for_missing_key
pytest tests/test_live_trading_db.py -v passes
userStories.notes: v2 changes: (1) mode default changed from 'live' to 'dry_run' (safe default), (2) get_live_state/set_live_state signatures made explicit (INSERT OR REPLACE pattern), (3) method signatures detailed with param types, (4) test cases enumerated explicitly. Follow exact _init_db() pattern: cursor.execute with triple-quoted SQL.

userStories.title: LiveOrderManager 모듈 — 브로커 주문 래퍼 및 생명주기 관리
userStories.description: As a live trader, I need an order manager that wraps broker.create_order() with lifecycle tracking, error handling, and fill confirmation so that orders are reliably executed and tracked.
acceptanceCriteria: Create trading_bot/live_order_manager.py with LiveOrder dataclass and LiveOrderManager class

LiveOrder fields: internal_id (str, uuid4), broker_order_id (Optional[str]), symbol (str), side (str 'buy'|'sell'), order_type (str 'market'|'limit'), requested_amount (float), requested_price (Optional[float]), filled_amount (float=0.0), filled_price (float=0.0), status (str='pending'), reason (str='signal'), submitted_at (datetime), filled_at (Optional[datetime]=None), commission (float=0.0), slippage_pct (float=0.0), error_message (Optional[str]=None)
LiveOrderManager.__init__(broker: BaseBroker, safety_guard: SafetyGuard, db: Optional[TradingDatabase]=None, notifier: Optional[NotificationService]=None, session_id: Optional[str]=None)

submit_order(symbol, side, amount, order_type='market', price=None, reason='signal', dry_run=False, positions=None, capital=0.0) -> LiveOrder — positions and capital passed to safety_guard.pre_order_check(). Flow: (1) dedup check (reject if same symbol+side within 5 seconds), (2) safety_guard.pre_order_check(symbol, side, amount, price, positions or {}, capital), (3) if dry_run: log and return with status='dry_run', (4) broker.create_order(symbol, order_type, side, amount, price), (5) log to DB, (6) poll fill status, (7) return LiveOrder

Broker status normalization: broker.fetch_order() and broker.create_order() may return status='closed'|'filled'|'open'|'canceled'. Normalize: 'closed'|'filled' -> treat as filled; 'open'|'pending'|'submitted' -> treat as pending; 'canceled'|'cancelled' -> treat as canceled

Fill polling: after broker.create_order(), call broker.fetch_order(broker_order_id, symbol) up to 5 times with 1-second sleep (market orders) or return immediately without polling (limit orders, caller checks later). If normalized status is 'filled': update LiveOrder with filled_amount, filled_price, filled_at, status='filled'. If still pending after 5 polls: set status='submitted', log warning

Slippage calc: only for filled orders with requested_price not None. slippage_pct = abs(filled_price - requested_price) / requested_price. Call safety_guard.post_fill_check(requested_price, filled_price)

Deduplication: internal _last_orders dict mapping 'symbol_side' -> timestamp. Reject if same key within 5 seconds (same pattern as OrderExecutor._is_duplicate_order). Return LiveOrder with status='rejected', error_message='Duplicate order within 5 seconds'

Error handling: InsufficientFunds -> status='failed', error_message=str(e), notify. RateLimitExceeded -> use retry_utils.retry_with_backoff (backoff_factor=2, max_retries=3, initial_delay=1). After 3 retries exhausted: status='failed', error_message='RateLimitExceeded after 3 retries'. BrokerError -> status='failed', error_message=str(e)[:500]. All errors: notifier.notify_error() if notifier

cancel_order(internal_id) -> bool — retrieve LiveOrder from _active_orders or DB. If broker_order_id is None: return False (order never placed). If broker_order_id exists: broker.cancel_order(broker_order_id, symbol). Update DB status='canceled'. Return True on success, False on failure
cancel_all_orders() -> int — iterate _active_orders with status in ('pending', 'submitted'), cancel each. Returns count of successfully canceled orders
get_pending_orders() -> List[LiveOrder] — returns orders from _active_orders with status in ('pending', 'submitted')
Import guards: from trading_bot.brokers.base_broker import BaseBroker, BrokerError, InsufficientFunds, RateLimitExceeded

Create tests/test_live_order_manager.py: test_successful_market_order (mock broker.create_order returns id, mock fetch_order returns filled), test_dry_run_no_broker_call (verify broker.create_order not called), test_insufficient_funds (mock raises InsufficientFunds, verify status='failed'), test_rate_limit_retry (mock raises RateLimitExceeded twice then succeeds), test_fill_polling_timeout (mock fetch_order always returns 'open', verify status='submitted' after 5 polls), test_slippage_calc (requested=100, filled=102, verify slippage_pct=0.02), test_cancel_order_success, test_cancel_order_no_broker_id (verify returns False), test_dedup_rejection (two orders same symbol+side within 1 second, second rejected)
pytest tests/test_live_order_manager.py -v passes
userStories.notes: v2 changes: (1) deduplication added (5-second window, same as OrderExecutor pattern), (2) broker status normalization added ('closed'/'filled' -> filled), (3) fill polling increased from 3 to 5 attempts for market orders, (4) limit orders skip polling (caller checks later), (5) cancel_order handles missing broker_order_id, (6) retry uses retry_utils pattern, (7) positions and capital added to submit_order params (passed to safety_guard), (8) error_message truncated to 500 chars. LiveOrderManager does NOT manage positions — only orders.

userStories.title: LiveTrader 코어 클래스 — 실제 주문 실행 트레이더
userStories.description: As a trader, I need a LiveTrader class that mirrors PaperTrader's signal loop but executes real orders through the broker, so that I can automate live trading with safety guards.
acceptanceCriteria: Create trading_bot/live_trader.py with LiveTrader class

Constructor params (order matches PaperTrader then adds live-specific): strategy (BaseStrategy), symbols (Union[str, List[str]]), broker (BaseBroker, REQUIRED), initial_capital=10000.0, position_size=0.95, commission=0.001, stop_loss_pct=0.05, take_profit_pct=0.10, enable_stop_loss=True, enable_take_profit=True, display_name=None, regime_detector=None, llm_client=None, notifier=None, db=None, mode='dry_run' (str), max_daily_loss_pct=0.05, max_daily_trades=50, max_position_count=10, order_type='market'

Internal component creation in __init__: self._safety_guard = SafetyGuard(initial_capital=initial_capital, max_daily_loss_pct=..., max_daily_trades=..., max_position_count=..., db=db, notifier=notifier). self._order_manager = LiveOrderManager(broker=broker, safety_guard=self._safety_guard, db=db, notifier=notifier). self._portfolio = PortfolioManager(symbols=self.symbols, initial_capital=initial_capital). self._signal_pipeline = SignalPipeline(regime_detector=regime_detector, llm_client=llm_client). self._risk_manager = RiskManager(stop_loss_pct=..., take_profit_pct=..., enable_stop_loss=..., enable_take_profit=...). self._perf_calc = PerformanceCalculator(). Do NOT use OrderExecutor

run_realtime(interval_seconds=60, timeframe='1h') — while not _stop_event.is_set(): for symbol in self.symbols: self._realtime_iteration(symbol, timeframe). Then time.sleep(interval_seconds). On exception: log error, increment _symbol_error_counts[symbol]. Same threading pattern as PaperTrader (_stop_event, _loop_exited)

_realtime_iteration(symbol, timeframe) — (1) broker.fetch_ticker(symbol, overseas=True) with CircuitBreaker, (2) broker.fetch_ohlcv(symbol, timeframe, limit=100, overseas=True) with CircuitBreaker, (3) strategy.get_current_signal(df) -> (signal, info), (4) self._signal_pipeline.process(signal=signal, symbol=symbol, df=df, info=info, timestamp=datetime.now(), positions=self._portfolio.positions, capital=self._portfolio.capital, initial_capital=self.initial_capital, strategy_name=self.strategy.name, db=self.db, session_id=self.session_id) -> (filtered_signal, regime), (5) risk_manager.check_positions() for stop_loss/take_profit -> force sell via _order_manager if triggered, (6) if signal changed and should trade: _order_manager.submit_order(symbol=symbol, side='buy'|'sell', amount=calc_quantity, order_type=self._order_type, price=current_price, reason='signal'|'stop_loss'|'take_profit', dry_run=(self._mode=='dry_run'), positions=self._portfolio.positions, capital=self._portfolio.capital)

On filled buy order: self._portfolio.positions[symbol] = order.filled_amount, self._portfolio.entry_prices[symbol] = order.filled_price (direct attribute access, same as PaperTrader pattern). On filled sell order: pnl = (order.filled_price - entry_price) * order.filled_amount, self._safety_guard.record_trade(pnl), clear positions[symbol]

Kill switch check: at start of _realtime_iteration(), if self._safety_guard.is_kill_switch_active(): log warning, skip iteration. Kill switch does NOT auto-close positions (documented limitation for MVP). User must manually close via broker

start() — session_id = f'live_{self.strategy.name.replace(" ","_")}_{int(time.time())}'. If db: db.create_live_session(session_id, strategy_name, display_name, mode, initial_capital, broker.name, broker.market_type). notifier.notify_session_start() if notifier

stop() — metrics = self._perf_calc.get_performance_summary(trades, equity_history, initial_capital). If db: db.update_live_session(session_id, {final_capital, total_return, sharpe_ratio, max_drawdown, win_rate, status='completed'|'killed', end_time, kill_switch_reason}). notifier.notify_session_end() if notifier. Set _loop_exited

CircuitBreaker: create self._ticker_cb = CircuitBreaker(failure_threshold=5, timeout=120) and self._ohlcv_cb = CircuitBreaker(failure_threshold=5, timeout=120). Same pattern as PaperTrader (param name is 'timeout' not 'recovery_timeout' per retry_utils.py)

Create tests/test_live_trader.py: test_init_creates_all_components (verify SafetyGuard, LiveOrderManager, PortfolioManager, SignalPipeline, RiskManager created), test_dry_run_signal_to_order (mock strategy returns BUY, verify _order_manager.submit_order called with dry_run=True), test_stop_loss_triggers_sell (mock position with 10% loss, verify sell order submitted with reason='stop_loss'), test_kill_switch_skips_iteration (activate kill switch, verify no orders placed), test_session_lifecycle (start creates session in DB, stop updates with metrics), test_filled_order_updates_portfolio (mock filled order, verify portfolio positions updated with fill price not signal price)
pytest tests/test_live_trader.py -v passes
userStories.notes: v2 changes: (1) SafetyGuard created with initial_capital param, (2) SignalPipeline.process() full param list documented, (3) overseas=True always passed to KIS broker calls, (4) CircuitBreaker config explicit (failure_threshold=5, recovery_timeout=120s), (5) kill switch does NOT auto-close positions (documented MVP limitation), (6) session_id format uses strategy name with spaces replaced by underscore, (7) portfolio update uses direct attribute access (positions[symbol], entry_prices[symbol]), (8) regime_detector and llm_client are optional (None = skip), (9) OrderExecutor explicitly NOT used. broker is REQUIRED (not optional like PaperTrader). KIS_MOCK=true -> broker still makes API calls to 모의투자 but with mock credentials.

userStories.title: 스케줄러 통합 — 라이브 트레이딩 잡 등록
userStories.description: As a system operator, I need the scheduler to support live trading sessions alongside paper trading so that live trading runs automatically during market hours.
acceptanceCriteria: In trading_bot/scheduler/scheduler_state.py: add to SchedulerContext.__init__(): self.active_live_traders: Dict = {}, self.live_trader_threads: Dict = {}. Add module-level aliases after existing aliases: active_live_traders = ctx.active_live_traders, live_trader_threads = ctx.live_trader_threads

In trading_bot/scheduler/scheduler_state.py: add try/except import: try: from trading_bot.live_trader import LiveTrader; _has_live_trader = True except ImportError: LiveTrader = None; _has_live_trader = False (after existing optional imports block)

In trading_bot/scheduler/session_manager.py: add _start_single_live_session(label: str, config: Dict) -> None — parallel to _start_single_session(). Creates LiveTrader with: mode=os.getenv('LIVE_TRADING_MODE', 'dry_run'), max_daily_loss_pct=float(os.getenv('LIVE_TRADING_MAX_DAILY_LOSS_PCT', '0.05')), etc. LIVE_TRADING_MODE validated: if not in ('dry_run', 'live') -> default 'dry_run'. Spawns daemon thread f'live-trader-{label}'. Stores in ctx.active_live_traders[label] and ctx.live_trader_threads[label]

In trading_bot/scheduler/session_manager.py: add start_live_trading() -> None — (1) if not _has_live_trader: return, (2) if os.getenv('LIVE_TRADING_ENABLED','false').lower() != 'true': return, (3) check kill switch: if ctx.global_db.get_live_state('kill_switch_active') == 'true': logger.warning('Kill switch active, skipping live trading'); return, (4) if not _is_trading_day(): return, (5) load same preset configs as paper trading, (6) _create_kis_broker() reusing ctx.global_broker, (7) for each config: _start_single_live_session(label, config)

In trading_bot/scheduler/session_manager.py: add stop_live_trading() -> None — parallel to stop_paper_trading(). Iterates ctx.active_live_traders: calls trader.stop(), thread.join(timeout=30), generates reports via ReportGenerator, uploads to Slack, clears ctx.active_live_traders and ctx.live_trader_threads

In scheduler.py: after paper trading jobs, add: if os.getenv('LIVE_TRADING_ENABLED', 'false').lower() == 'true': add_job(start_live_trading, cron, same market hours as paper trading). add_job(stop_live_trading, cron, same close time)

Add to .env.example in new '# --- Live Trading ---' section: LIVE_TRADING_ENABLED=false, LIVE_TRADING_MODE=dry_run (comment: 'dry_run' logs orders without submitting; 'live' submits real orders), LIVE_TRADING_MAX_DAILY_LOSS_PCT=0.05, LIVE_TRADING_MAX_DAILY_TRADES=50, LIVE_TRADING_MAX_POSITION_COUNT=10. Add comment: 'KIS_MOCK=true overrides at API level — all orders go to 모의투자 regardless of LIVE_TRADING_MODE'
Live trading and paper trading run simultaneously using separate state dicts (active_live_traders vs active_traders). They share ctx.global_broker instance (avoid duplicate API connections)
pytest tests/ -k 'not test_live_trader and not test_live_order_manager and not test_safety_guard and not test_live_trading_db' passes (no regression to existing tests)
userStories.notes: v2 changes: (1) LIVE_TRADING_MODE validated with fallback to 'dry_run', (2) kill switch checked before starting sessions, (3) KIS_MOCK vs LIVE_TRADING_MODE precedence documented (.env.example comment), (4) thread naming f'live-trader-{label}' to distinguish from paper, (5) report generation uses same ReportGenerator pattern as stop_paper_trading, (6) global_broker shared between paper and live (not separate instances), (7) DST handling reuses existing _is_trading_day() and market hour logic (already DST-aware). Presets are shared between paper and live — mode param distinguishes execution behavior.

userStories.title: 모듈 등록 및 문서화
userStories.description: As a developer, I need LiveTrader and SafetyGuard properly exported and documented so that they are discoverable and usable.
acceptanceCriteria: In trading_bot/__init__.py: add single try/except block (flat, not nested) after PaperTrader import block: try: from .safety_guard import SafetyGuard; from .live_order_manager import LiveOrderManager, LiveOrder; from .live_trader import LiveTrader; _has_live_trader = True except ImportError: SafetyGuard = LiveOrderManager = LiveOrder = LiveTrader = None; _has_live_trader = False
After the try/except block: if _has_live_trader: __all__.extend(['SafetyGuard', 'LiveOrderManager', 'LiveOrder', 'LiveTrader'])
Update root CLAUDE.md Phase 5 section: change '계획중' to '진행중 (MVP)', add bullet items: SafetyGuard (킬스위치+일일한도), LiveOrderManager (브로커 주문 래퍼), LiveTrader (실제 주문 실행), 스케줄러 통합 (LIVE_TRADING_ENABLED)
Verify: python -c 'from trading_bot import LiveTrader, SafetyGuard, LiveOrderManager, LiveOrder; print("OK")' prints OK
pytest tests/ passes (full suite including new test files, no regression)
userStories.notes: v2 changes: (1) flat try/except (not nested) for simpler error handling — if any import fails, all are None, (2) CLAUDE.md update scope clarified (root CLAUDE.md, Phase 5 section), (3) import verification command made explicit. Import order within try block: SafetyGuard (no deps) -> LiveOrderManager (depends on SafetyGuard) -> LiveTrader (depends on both). Circular import risk: None — all 3 modules import from existing trading_bot submodules, not from each other's __init__.
